title: Mr. 3 Studies the Trap
description: On pages 14–17, mr. 3 studies the trap as the battle between Dorry and Brogy is poisoned by interference, pushing Luffy and Usopp toward a furious confrontation.
scene: Mr. 3 Studies the Trap. The battle between Dorry and Brogy is poisoned by interference, pushing Luffy and Usopp toward a furious confrontation. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Gegya Gyagya Gya!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 3
dialogue.text: Then why don't you just test your luck and sail ahead!? If fate wills it, you'll reach your destination!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Dahaha haha!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: You think!?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 3
dialogue.text: You know, it just might work!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: gegyagyagagagagagaya! You really are one interesting little human!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Gegya Gyagya Gya

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 3
dialogue.text: Umm...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: A brave warrior of the seas?

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: What's that?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 3
dialogue.text: It's someone like you! I want to become just like you two one day!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: A giant?

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: No, that's not what I mean!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: I want to live proud and honourably,

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Just like the warriors of Elbaf!

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Gabababa! I see!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: We Giants have much longer lifespans than you humans,

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: And as such, we wish nothing more than an honourable death.

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: After all,

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: One's fortunes or life is all bound to come to an end one day.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: to a warrior of Elbaf,

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: to die without tarnishing one's pride is what we mean by an honourable death.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 3
dialogue.text: Our pride alone never comes to an end.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: It's an eternal treasure that's passed on through the lands of Elbaf.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: So your pride is your treasure!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: The rum...

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: explored!.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Mr. Giant!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Mr. DORRY!

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on mr. 3 studies the trap. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
